evidence: Study 1 used "a permutation-based feature importance analysis" that "shuffled the values of each of the 594 predictors one at a time across all observations and assessed the extent to which the model's loss value changed due to this permutation." The top 60 items from this ranking form the ML-CVI. However, Studies 3a-4b validate the explanatory conclusions through mediation analysis: "In study 3a, we found that among five prominent existing culture scales and 60 items of the ML-CVI, only ML-CVI item 26 was able to explain the difference between Americans' and Mexicans' behaviors in following Covid-19 lockdown orders." Similarly, final conclusions about perseverance explaining US-India environmental differences are corroborated via direct mediation testing, not feature importance magnitude.
